verdict: Moderate risk of AI displacement, but human judgment and empathy are essential for patient care.

reasoning_steps.title: Increasing automation in administrative tasks
reasoning_steps.body: AI can already perform routine administrative tasks, such as scheduling appointments and processing medical records.

reasoning_steps.title: Limited AI capability gap for complex decision-making
reasoning_steps.body: While AI has made significant progress in medical diagnosis, human doctors are still necessary for complex decision-making and nuanced clinical judgment.

adjacent_careers.name: Physician Assistant
adjacent_careers.reasoning: Physician assistants work under the supervision of doctors and can perform routine tasks, but human judgment is still essential for complex decision-making.

adjacent_careers.name: Nurse Practitioner
adjacent_careers.reasoning: Nurse practitioners can provide primary care services, but human doctors are necessary for more complex cases and nuanced clinical judgment.

recent_developments.headline: AI-assisted diagnosis gains traction in medical field
recent_developments.impact: AI-assisted diagnosis is becoming more prevalent, but human doctors are still necessary for complex decision-making.

recent_developments.headline: FDA clears more AI diagnostic tools each quarter
recent_developments.impact: Radiology, pathology, and dermatology are seeing AI second-readers move into routine clinical workflows.

quotes.quote: AI in medicine should be evaluated like a drug — for safety, for efficacy, and for the population it actually helps.
quotes.speaker: Eric Topol
quotes.title: Cardiologist; Director, Scripps Research Translational Institute

quotes.quote: We should stop training radiologists now. It is just completely obvious that within five years, deep learning is going to do better than radiologists.
quotes.speaker: Geoffrey Hinton
quotes.title: Turing Award laureate; ex-Google
quotes.source: Machine Learning and the Market for Intelligence conference, Toronto

quotes.quote: AI will not replace doctors, but doctors who use AI will replace doctors who don't.
quotes.speaker: Curtis Langlotz
quotes.title: Professor of Radiology and Biomedical Informatics, Stanford
